question: Q6
summary: fsrs.ts rank-time structural-freshness is a pure write-free function: computeStructuralFreshness(stability,centrality)=stability*centrality; computeGraphCentrality is pure — no DB handle, no persist

question: Q6
summary: The ONLY write-back is strengthenOnAccess UPDATE memory_index SET stability,last_review,review_count+1,access_count+1,last_accessed — it is FSRS reinforcement (testing effect, low-R memories get biggest boost), NOT decay computation

question: Q6
summary: Rank-time effective R(t) is already computed independently BEFORE the write: applyTestingEffect reads stored stability+last_review, computes elapsedDays + currentR=calculateRetrievability(stability,elapsedDays), THEN calls strengthenOnAccess — split is already structurally half-present

question: Q6
summary: Write-back is trackAccess-gated (opt-in), so default read-only recall already NEVER writes back — never-written-back behavior already exists on the common path; divergence from aionforge is the opt-in write exists at all and uses ambient CURRENT_TIMESTAMP/Date.now() not a caller clock

question: Q6
summary: Naive remove-write-back is NOT safe: the write-back is a used learning signal (review_count/access_count/last_review + difficulty-weighted stability boost) that feeds the next recall's calculateRetrievability and last_accessed recency — removing it makes decay monotonic and loses the testing effect; this is the one place internal genuinely diverges from aionforge's stance

question: Q7
summary: NO session/spec-folder dominance cap anywhere in search pipeline — grep for session.?divers|dominanc|spill|maxPerSession|maxPerFolder|maxPerSpec across lib/search/ handlers/ returns zero session/folder-cap logic; a single chatty session or dominant spec-folder can occupy the whole top-k
evidence: lib/search/ handlers/ grep session-diversity — no hits

question: Q7
summary: Result-assembly truncation is two bare slice(0,limit) steps with no per-source cap: stage-2 artifact-limit slice(0,strategy.maxResults) and stage-4 final slice(0,config.limit); stage-4 per-memory-state tier limits cap by lifecycle state, not session

question: Q7
summary: Existing MMR 'diversity' (apply_n / applyDiversity) is embedding-similarity diversity (mmr_score=relevance - n_factor*max_similarity_to_selected, intent-driven lambda), an ORTHOGONAL axis to aionforge session diversity which is explicitly an episode/provenance notion

question: Q7
summary: aionforge contract internal lacks: session_diversity_cap default 3 = most memories from one session into primary set before rest spill; spill appended only if bundle under-filled; cap is an episode notion, facts have no session and go straight to primary set in fused order

question: Q1/Q2
summary: Per-channel weight seam for a future query-class router is RankedList.weight?:number consumed by fuseResultsMulti; a class router would emit a per-class weight vector and populate each channel weight pre-fusion, honoring weight:0 to elide a channel (aionforge zero-weight parity)

question: Q6
summary: Split decay into (a) pure rank-time effective-score, always-on, caller-clock (promote existing calculateRetrievability to every recall, read elapsed time vs caller-supplied nowMs not ambient clock) and (b) reinforcement as a separate optional explicitly-emitted event (keep strengthenOnAccess but fire only on deliberate 'memory was used' signal, decoupled from rank order) — gets aionforge determinism WITHOUT losing the testing effect

question: Q7
summary: Insert session/spec-folder diversity cap immediately before the stage-4 final slice: walk score-ordered list, admit at most N (default 3) rows per spec_folder/sessionId, overflow to spill list, append spill only if primary set under config.limit (aionforge spill-if-underfilled). In-place partition before existing slice, no scoring change. Open: cap key configurable (spec_folder primary), and a memory-type exemption since internal has no episode/fact split

question: Q5
summary: Incidental: a sortDeterministicRows step exists at stage2-fusion.ts:1387 immediately before truncation — relevant to Q5 render-order determinism (iter-3 C5) but out of iter-4 focus; flag for Q5 follow-up to check whether its tie-break is content-derived

question: Q6
summary: Q6 residual: caller-clock threading (where nowMs enters pipeline config so decay AND any bi-temporal filter read the same caller instant) not yet traced; ties into Q3 bi-temporal axis and aionforge single caller-provided instant
